target: batched f64 matmul via Primitive::DotGeneral (bMK,bKN->bMN; attention/batched-GEMM), bench eval/dot_general_batched_8x256x256x256_f64
function: tensor_contraction::batched_matmul_2d (threaded over flattened batch×row space, fast i-k-j kernel) + arithmetic::batched_standard_f64_matmul routing in eval_dot_general
lever: Route canonical batched f64 matmul off the generic per-(output,contracted)-pair dot_general loop onto a contiguous i-k-j kernel parallelized over the flattened (batch × output-row) space. Combines the fast kernel (~10x better per-FMA than the per-element loop) with full multi-core utilization regardless of batch count. The pass117 lever threaded the SLOW general kernel; this uses the FAST kernel. Gated >=1M FMAs (routing) and >=67M (threading); small batched matmuls keep the LLVM-optimized generic loop.
isomorphism: BIT-EXACT. Each output element accumulates a[b,i,l]*b[b,l,j] in ascending-l order at batch-major row-major position, identical to the serial per-batch matmul / generic dot_general loop. Proven by batched_matmul_2d_bit_identical (kernel vs textbook) + dot_general_parallel_bit_identical (4x256^3=67.1M end-to-end via the routed path, to_bits vs textbook per-batch ref) + 6/6 dot_general & 12/12 matmul tests.
note: 8x256x256x256 (134M FMAs): generic threaded loop (pass117) 114.37ms -> fast kernel 7.3238ms = ~17x worker-corrected; vs the original serial general loop (6.26s, pass117 evidence) ~855x. ~18 GFLOP/s effective (fast i-k-j + ~48-64 core RCH worker).
score: impact 5 (batched matmul = attention, the hottest reachable ML op) x confidence 5 (bit-exact, multiple tests, serial path untouched) / effort 3 = 8.3
tests: fj-lax matmul 12/12 + dot_general 6/6 (incl batched_matmul_2d_bit_identical, dot_general_parallel_bit_identical); clippy -D warnings clean.
bench_note: Measured via temporary bench in foreign-dirty lax_baseline.rs (restored, not committed). Baseline via in-place BATCHED_FASTPATH_MIN_OPS=usize::MAX toggle (-> threaded general loop). Only arithmetic.rs + tensor_contraction.rs committed.
